steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student's goal is to "list activities that the Heritage Trail can be used for." The answer must enumerate specific uses of the trail — not background facts about its origin or location.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Railroad corridors can be used to create trails, one of these being the Heritage Trail.: explains how the trail was formed, not what visitors do there. ✗
  • Choice B — The Heritage Trail was created from a former railroad corridor.: another origin detail, not an activity list. ✗
  • Choice C — The Heritage Trail can be used for biking, in-line skating, and cross-country skiing.: directly lists three activities visitors can do. ✓
  • Choice D — Located in New York, the Heritage Trail is open to visitors.: states location and access, not specific activities. ✗
That is the correct answer.
